methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Amber Bio is a Seed-stage biotech company founded by Basem Al-Shayeb and Jacob Borrajo that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across Biotech & Health (3 public, 8 private), including established players like Tempus AI, Abridge, and Ultima Genomics.

Only one valuation method proved applicable given data constraints. Stage-Progression methodology uses the company's $26M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement in biotech. This yields an implied valuation of $65M. Peer-Median triangulation was not feasible; while 16 same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage-aligned and tier-comparable companies yielded fewer than the three datapoints needed for statistical reliability. Public-Comp Anchoring was inapplicable because Amber Bio, as a private company, does not disclose revenue; the public biotech median P/S multiple of 5.8x across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L therefore cannot be operationalized.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $55M to $75M, centered at $65M. This range reflects typical progression economics for well-capitalized Seed companies in the biotech sector. The primary uncertainty stems from limited comparable transaction data at Amber Bio's specific stage and funding level, and from the absence of disclosed financial metrics that would enable revenue or cash-burn based valuation anchors.
